channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NOAA set to fire 1,029 more employees
item.description: Staffing levels at the nation's already hobbled weather, climate and oceans agency could take a further nosedive in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KTLA 5

item.title: Harvey Weinstein due in court for key rulings as his #MeToo retrial nears
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein 's #MeToo retrial next month will largely be an abridged version of the original, with one big addition: a charge based on an allegation from a woman who wasn’t a part of the first case.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil didn't wear a mask. He's now the face of Trump's crackdown on campus protests
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When protests over the Israel-Hamas war took root on Columbia University's campus last spring, Mahmoud Khalil became a familiar, outspoken figure in a student movement that soon spread to other U.S. colleges.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States should scale back abortion reporting demands, advocacy group says
item.description: An advocacy group says it's time for states to stop requiring abortion providers to file reports for every abortion.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports go into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump officially increased tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports to 25% on Wednesday, promising that the taxes would help create U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried easily wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of post-election drama over chamber control decisively chose Democrat David Gottfried on Tuesday, tying the House and ending a short-lived Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis follows Vatican spiritual retreat as doctors say he's no longer in imminent danger
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis participated remotely in the Vatican's spiritual retreat Tuesday after getting good news from his doctors: They upgraded his prognosis and say he is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of the respiratory infection that has kept him hospitalized for nearly a mon
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney's offices around the country, two people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AG concludes charges in gang-related Nevada prison brawl that left 3 inmates dead
item.description: Twenty people have been charged in connection to a 2024 Nevada prison brawl that ended with three dead inmates, authorities said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: Jamie White, an Infowars employee, was the victim of a homicide in southeast Austin, Texas, late Sunday night, law enforcement sources confirmed to Nexstar's KXAN.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, Fox 5

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership on Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote, to be sure, was not decisive, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Police release more details on murder of InfoWars reporter Jamie White as search for suspects continues
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas, believe InfoWars reporter Jamie White was shot and killed when he interrupted suspects possibly burglarizing his car at his apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KRON4

item.title: A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first nitrogen gas execution. The state says it will appeal
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge has halted Louisiana’s first death row execution using nitrogen gas, which was scheduled to take place next week. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is known about the deadly January air crash between a passenger jet and US Army helicopter
item.description: Federal aviation officials will continue making sure that planes and helicopters no longer share the same airspace over the nation's capital in the wake of January's midair collision, which killed 67 people and was the deadliest U.S. plane crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia protester arrested by ICE and facing deportation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Palestinian activist who helped lead student protests at Columbia University faces deportation after being arrested over the weekend by federal immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing American college student seen on resort surveillance in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki, the missing American college student, was captured on surveillance footage walking with a group of friends in the last known image of her before she disappeared.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tiger Woods says he ruptured his Achilles tendon
item.description: It's yet another injury that would seem likely to keep him out of the Masters and perhaps other major championships this year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas measles outbreak rises to 223 cases
item.description: The measles outbreak in the South Plains region of Texas has grown to 223 cases. 29 of those infected have been hospitalized and one school-aged child has died, according to a new update from the Texas Department of State Health Services.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NTSB calls for ban on some helicopter routes near Reagan Airport after midair collision that killed 67 people
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board is calling for a ban on some helicopter flights near Reagan Airport after an airliner and Army Black Hawk collided in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada electricity tariffs to target 3 US states
item.description: "If the United States escalates, I will not hesitate to shut the electricity off completely," said Doug Ford.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: USDA halts more than $1B in funding for local food banks, schools
item.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) says it has nixed more than $1 billion in funding for local food banks and schools by terminating two programs that aid state, tribal and territorial government food purchases.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Family of missing college student asks authorities to broaden search
item.description: DOMINICAN REPUBLIC (WFLA) — The family of a University of Pittsburgh college student who went missing in Punta Cana is asking authorities to broaden their search. Sudiksha Konanki was last seen on March 6 with a young man that authorities are speaking with.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Abbey Gate terror suspect remains in custody, faces charges connected to deaths of 13 US service members
item.description: Suspected Abbey Gate terror plotter Mohammad Sharifullah appeared in federal court on Monday, where the judge ordered he be held pending further hearings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Arizona prisoner set to be executed next week passes up chance to ask for reprieve
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A prisoner scheduled to be executed next week in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years has passed up a chance to ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rubio confirms ICE arrest of pro-Palestinian Columbia student protest leader
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has confirmed the arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a green card-holding Columbia University graduate and pro-Palestinian activist, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks plunge as Wall Street wonders how much economic pain Trump will accept
item.description: The economy has already given some signals of weakening, mostly through surveys showing increased pessimism.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Texas district attorney declines to pursue charges against Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver Xavier Worthy
item.description: Xavier Worthy was arrested Friday and has since been released after the county's district attorney said he would not accept the case at this time.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Robert Pattison sci-fi ‘Mickey 17’ opens in first place, but profitability is a long way off
item.description: “Parasite” filmmaker Bong Joon Ho’s original science fiction film “Mickey 17” opened in first place on the North American box office charts.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5
